pattern: Sorting lets you check only adjacent triples from the largest end, because if the two next-largest sides cannot beat the current biggest side no smaller pair ever will.
hints: Three lengths form a triangle exactly when the two shorter ones together exceed the longest; equality gives zero area and does not count.
To maximize the perimeter, prefer the largest possible sides, so scan from the top of a sorted array.
Sort ascending; sweep i from the back and test nums[i-2] + nums[i-1] > nums[i]. The first triple that passes is the answer; if none do, return 0.

edge_inputs.description: exactly three sides forming a degenerate (zero-area) triangle
largest triple wins over a smaller valid one
huge dominant side prevents any triangle

stress.description: random side lengths over a wide range
stress.note: mix often yields a valid triple but sometimes none
